message:
feat(replay): implement .ftreplay chunked archive format with compression

Massive expansion of the replay fixture harvest pipeline (+2,220 lines)
implementing the full .ftreplay file format specification:

Archive format (ftreplay.v1):
- Section-based file layout: header → entities → timeline → decisions → footer
- Chunked timeline storage with configurable max events per chunk
  (DEFAULT_MAX_EVENTS_PER_CHUNK = 100,000)
- Per-chunk gzip compression via flate2 for efficient disk storage
- Content-addressable chunk manifests (ftreplay.chunk_manifest.v1)

New capabilities:
- FtreplayWriter: streaming archive writer that accepts events and
  flushes chunks incrementally, supporting arbitrarily large captures
- FtreplayReader: random-access reader with chunk-level decompression,
  entity index lookups, and timeline range queries
- SensitivityTier::as_ftreplay_label(): compact tier labels (T1/T2/T3)
  for archive metadata
- Invariant checking integration: validates captured events against
  recorder_invariants (ViolationKind/ViolationSeverity) during harvest
- BTreeMap-based entity registry for deterministic serialization order
- VecDeque-backed streaming event buffer for memory-bounded processing

Constants:
- FTREPLAY_FILE_SCHEMA_VERSION, FTREPLAY_CHUNK_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION
- Section markers for header/entities/timeline/decisions/footer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
